hypothesis: We hypothesize that augmenting SAM-EM with an explicit temporal coherence module (e.g., optical-flow-informed memory or a lightweight temporal transformer) that enforces trajectory-consistent masks across frames will reduce particle identity switches by at least 40% and increase the J&F score by at least 12 percentage points on thick-liquid (≥100 nm) LPTEM sequences with frequent particle overlaps, compared to SAM-EM without the temporal module, while preserving near-real-time inference (≥20 fps) on a standard GPU.
